title: Zero test files in project
detection_signal: No files matching patterns: **/*test*, **/*spec*, **/tests/**, **/__tests__/**, **/test/** in entire repo; exclude node_modules, vendor, .venv
why_demo: No automated verification whatsoever. The developer 'tested' by running the app once and hoping for the best. Any refactoring is Russian roulette.

title: Test-to-source ratio below threshold
detection_signal: Count of test files / count of source files < 0.3 (excluding config, generated, and vendored files)
why_demo: Most code paths are unverified. Even if some tests exist, the coverage is token — enough to show a green badge, not enough to catch real bugs.

title: No test runner configured
detection_signal: No 'test' script in package.json, no pytest.ini/setup.cfg/pyproject.toml [tool.pytest], no build.gradle test task, no Cargo.toml [dev-dependencies], no go test invocation in Makefile/CI
why_demo: Tests may exist as files but have no mechanism to actually run them. The test files are decorative — exist to impress code reviewers, never executed.

title: No coverage tooling configured
detection_signal: No nyc/c8/istanbul in devDependencies; no --coverage in test script; no pytest-cov in requirements; no coverage.xml or lcov references in CI config; no .coveragerc
why_demo: Without coverage metrics, there's no accountability. The team has no idea which code paths are tested and which are time bombs.

title: Coverage threshold not enforced
detection_signal: Coverage tool configured but no threshold in config (no 'threshold' in jest.config, no --cov-fail-under in pytest, no coverage.minimum in vitest.config); OR threshold set below 60%
why_demo: Coverage can rot silently. Without an enforced floor, 'we'll add tests later' becomes 'we never added tests' within a sprint.

title: Tests only cover happy path
detection_signal: Test files contain no assertions for error states: absence of try/catch in tests, no .rejects/.toThrow/.raises, no 4xx/5xx status assertions, no negative test case names (grep for 'error|fail|invalid|reject|throw|exception' in test names yields < 10% of test count)
why_demo: Happy-path-only tests verify the demo scenario. Production is the unhappy path — invalid inputs, timeouts, partial failures. These tests catch zero production issues.

title: No edge case / boundary testing
detection_signal: No property-based testing libraries (hypothesis, fast-check, quickcheck); no fuzz testing; test inputs are all single hardcoded values with no null/empty/zero/max/negative/unicode variants
why_demo: Demo code works with the three test values the developer hardcoded. It has never encountered an empty array, a null field, a 10MB payload, or a unicode string.

title: No integration tests
detection_signal: All test files contain only unit-test patterns (mock/stub/spy in >80% of tests); no test files in directories named integration/e2e/smoke; no HTTP client usage in tests (axios/fetch/requests in test files); no test database setup/teardown
why_demo: Every dependency is mocked to hell. The tests prove each function works in isolation but say nothing about whether the system actually functions. The mocks reflect what the developer *thinks* the dependencies do, not what they *actually* do.

title: No end-to-end tests
detection_signal: No playwright/cypress/selenium/puppeteer in devDependencies or config files; no *.e2e.* or *.spec.e2e.* files; no E2E step in CI pipeline
why_demo: The user journey from UI to database has never been verified. Components may work individually but the wiring between them is untested.

title: No CI/CD pipeline
detection_signal: No .github/workflows/*.yml, no .gitlab-ci.yml, no Jenkinsfile, no .circleci/config.yml, no azure-pipelines.yml, no bitbucket-pipelines.yml, no .buildkite/, no cloudbuild.yaml
why_demo: Deployment is 'works on my machine.' There is no automated gate between a developer pushing code and it hitting production. Quality is a gentleman's agreement.

title: CI pipeline has no test step
detection_signal: CI config exists but contains no 'test'/'pytest'/'npm test'/'go test' step; CI only runs build/compile, not test
why_demo: CI exists for show — it builds and deploys but never validates. The pipeline is a conveyor belt shipping untested code to production.

title: CI tests are not required to pass for merge
detection_signal: GitHub branch protection rules missing required status checks; CI step present but not in 'required' checks; 'allow merge when pipeline fails' enabled in GitLab; no CODEOWNERS file
why_demo: Tests are advisory. A failing test is a yellow warning that developers learn to ignore. Red CI becomes background noise.

title: No linting configuration
detection_signal: No .eslintrc*/eslint.config.*, no .pylintrc/pyproject.toml [tool.pylint], no .flake8/pyproject.toml [tool.flake8], no .golangci.yml, no .rubocop.yml, no checkstyle.xml, no .clippy.toml in project root
why_demo: Code style and bug patterns are caught only by human review (or not at all). Automated static analysis that catches entire classes of bugs is simply absent.

title: Linting not enforced in CI
detection_signal: Lint config exists but no lint step in CI pipeline; or lint step present but 'allow_failure'/'continue-on-error: true'
why_demo: Linting is opt-in, which means it's opt-out. Developers run it when they remember, skip it when they're in a hurry (always).

title: No type checking enforcement
detection_signal: TSConfig missing 'strict: true' or has 'noImplicitAny: false' or 'strictNullChecks: false'; no mypy/mypy.ini/pyproject.toml [tool.mypy]; no flow config; 'any' type usage > 5% of type annotations
why_demo: The type system is decoration. 'any' is the escape hatch that turns TypeScript into JavaScript with extra steps. Null pointer exceptions are just waiting for production traffic.

title: Excessive use of 'any' type
detection_signal: Ratio of ': any' or 'as any' occurrences to total type annotations > 10%; or > 20 occurrences of ': any' in codebase
why_demo: Every 'any' is a hole in the type safety net. It means the developer gave up on describing the data shape — and the compiler can no longer catch mistakes.

title: No pre-commit hooks
detection_signal: No .husky/, no .pre-commit-config.yaml, no lint-staged in package.json, no .git/hooks/ custom scripts, no lefthook.yml, no simple-git-hooks config
why_demo: Nothing prevents garbage from entering the repo. A developer can commit broken code, formatting errors, and console.log statements without any friction.

title: No lock file committed
detection_signal: No package-lock.json, yarn.lock, pnpm-lock.yaml in JS projects; no Pipfile.lock, poetry.lock, uv.lock in Python; no Gemfile.lock in Ruby; no Cargo.lock in Rust binaries; no go.sum in Go
why_demo: Dependency resolution is non-deterministic. Today it works because dependency v2.3.1 is installed. Tomorrow a new developer (or CI) gets v2.4.0 with a breaking change. 'Works on my machine' in lock-file form.

title: Lock file in .gitignore
detection_signal: .gitignore contains 'package-lock.json' or 'yarn.lock' or 'poetry.lock' or '*.lock' pattern
why_demo: Someone deliberately excluded the reproducibility mechanism. This is actively hostile to deployment consistency.

title: No dependency update strategy
detection_signal: No dependabot.yml/.github/dependabot.yml, no renovate.json/renovate.json5/.renovaterc, no greenkeeper.json, no Snyk config; AND project age > 90 days (check first commit date)
why_demo: Dependencies rot silently. Known CVEs accumulate. Eventually something breaks or gets exploited, and the team discovers their 'stable' codebase is running on ancient, vulnerable packages.

title: Pinned dependencies with no update mechanism
detection_signal: All dependencies pinned to exact versions (no ^ or ~ in package.json) AND no dependabot/renovate config
why_demo: Pinning without updating is not safety — it's stagnation. The codebase fossilizes on the versions available at project creation and accumulates unpatched vulnerabilities.

title: Manual deployment scripts
detection_signal: Deployment docs reference manual SSH commands, 'scp', 'rsync' to prod, 'docker push' in README; no deploy automation in CI; scripts in deploy/ or scripts/ contain hardcoded server IPs or hostnames
why_demo: Deployment requires tribal knowledge and manual steps. The one person who knows the incantation goes on vacation and nobody can ship. Every deploy is a potential production outage from a typo.

title: No rollback capability
detection_signal: No 'rollback' step in deployment pipeline; no versioned deployments; no blue-green or canary configuration; docker-compose uses 'latest' tag; no deployment history or version tags
why_demo: If the deploy breaks production, the recovery plan is 'fix forward' which at 3am with a broken database migration means hours of downtime. Demo code assumes nothing ever goes wrong.

title: No staging / pre-production environment
detection_signal: CI/CD deploys directly to production; no staging/preview/qa environment in pipeline config; no environment variables for different stages beyond 'production'
why_demo: Code goes from developer laptop to production with zero intermediate validation. The first real users are the quality assurance team.

title: Hotfixes pushed directly to main
detection_signal: Git history shows commits on main with 'hotfix'/'fix'/'urgent'/'patch' in message AND no corresponding PR/MR; or branch protection allows direct push to main
why_demo: Emergency bypasses become habitual. The 'just this once' hotfix skips tests, review, and staging. Each one is a gamble that becomes the norm.

title: No code review requirement
detection_signal: No required reviewers in branch protection; no CODEOWNERS file; PRs with 0 reviews merged in git log; merge button has no 'approvals required' gate
why_demo: A single developer's blind spots become production bugs. No second pair of eyes means design flaws, security holes, and logic errors ship undetected.

title: Tests depend on external services
detection_signal: Test files contain real URLs (https://api.stripe.com, https://*.amazonaws.com, https://api.openai.com); test files import HTTP clients without mocking; no test fixtures/mocks directory; test env contains real API keys
why_demo: Tests fail when the external service is down, rate-limited, or changed its API. Test reliability depends on third-party uptime. CI randomly fails and developers learn to ignore red builds.

title: No test data management
detection_signal: No factories (factory_bot, faker, fishery); no fixtures directory; no seed data scripts; tests create data inline with hardcoded strings; no test database reset between runs
why_demo: Test data is ad-hoc and fragile. Tests pass when run in order but fail when run in isolation (shared mutable state). Adding a new test breaks existing ones.

title: Snapshot tests without review process
detection_signal: Snapshot files (*.snap) exist AND git log shows commits with 'update snapshots' AND no snapshot review in PR checklist; snapshot files > 500 lines
why_demo: Snapshot tests became a rubber stamp. Developers run '--update-snapshot' instead of understanding what changed. The snapshots grew to thousands of lines nobody reads — false security.

title: Flaky test suppression
detection_signal: Test files contain retry/repeat annotations (.retry(3), flaky, @retries, pytest-rerunfailures); OR 'skip'/'xit'/'xtest'/'pending' with reason containing 'flaky|intermittent|sometimes|random'; OR CI config has 'retry' on test step
why_demo: Flaky tests are symptoms of real race conditions or non-deterministic behavior. Retrying them masks the underlying bug. Eventually it hits production and nobody can reproduce it.

title: Disabled or skipped tests
detection_signal: Count of skipped/disabled tests (xit, xdescribe, xtest, @Ignore, @Disabled, test.skip, pytest.mark.skip, t.Skip) > 5% of total test count; OR any skipped test with TODO/FIXME comment
why_demo: Disabled tests are broken promises. The code they were protecting is now unguarded. Every skipped test is a regression waiting to happen with nobody watching.

title: No format / prettier configuration
detection_signal: No .prettierrc*/prettier.config.*, no .editorconfig, no rustfmt.toml, no .clang-format, no black/pyproject.toml [tool.black]; AND multiple contributors exist
why_demo: Every file has different formatting. PRs are full of whitespace diffs that obscure real changes. Code review energy is wasted on 'please fix indentation' instead of logic.

title: No security scanning in pipeline
detection_signal: No Snyk, Trivy, Dependabot security alerts, CodeQL, Semgrep, Bandit, Safety, npm audit, or OWASP dependency-check in CI pipeline or project config
why_demo: Known vulnerabilities ship to production unchallenged. The codebase is a sitting duck for CVEs in transitive dependencies.

title: No secrets management
detection_signal: Hardcoded API keys/passwords/tokens in source code (regex: /(sk-|pk_|AKIA|ghp_|gho_|xoxb-|xoxp-)[a-zA-Z0-9]{20,}/); OR .env files committed to repo; OR secrets in CI config files as plaintext
why_demo: Secrets in code are one git push away from public exposure. When (not if) the repo leaks, every key is a backdoor into production infrastructure.

title: Console.log / print statement debugging left in
detection_signal: Source files (non-test) contain console.log/console.warn/console.debug, print(), println(), fmt.Println(), puts, echo (in non-CLI code), System.out.println(); count > 10 in production code
why_demo: Debug statements in production are noise, performance waste, and potential information leaks. They tell you the developer was debugging and forgot to clean up.

title: TODO/FIXME/HACK debt accumulation
detection_signal: Count of TODO/FIXME/HACK/XXX/TEMP/TEMPORARY/WORKAROUND comments > 15 in production code; OR any TODO older than 180 days (check git blame date)
why_demo: TODOs are deferred quality. When they accumulate, the codebase is held together with promises never kept. Each one is a known problem the team chose to ship anyway.

title: No documentation for setup / onboarding
detection_signal: No README.md at project root; OR README exists but contains no setup/install/quickstart section (< 50 words); no CONTRIBUTING.md; no docs/ directory for complex projects (> 20 source files)
why_demo: The project is a black box. New team members need oral tradition to contribute. Knowledge is trapped in the original developer's head.

title: No environment parity
detection_signal: No Dockerfile/docker-compose.yml for local dev; no .env.example; no Vagrantfile; dev dependencies differ from CI/production (different Node/Python version in CI vs local); no .tool-versions/.nvmrc/runtime.txt
why_demo: Dev, CI, and production run different configurations. 'It works locally' is meaningless when the environments don't match. Bugs appear only in specific environments.

title: No monorepo / workspace dependency management
detection_signal: Multiple package.json or pyproject.toml in subdirectories with duplicate dependency declarations and version drift (same package at different versions across workspaces); no workspace protocol or turborepo/nx/lerna config
why_demo: Dependency versions drift across packages. One module uses lodash 4.17.20 with a security patch, another uses 4.17.15. Inconsistent runtime behavior across the codebase.

title: No error boundary / global error handling
detection_signal: No React error boundaries in component tree; no global exception handler (Express error middleware, Flask errorhandler, Spring @ControllerAdvice); no Sentry/Bugsnag/Rollbar integration; unhandled rejection handlers absent
why_demo: Errors crash the entire application instead of being contained. One bad component takes down the whole UI. One unhandled promise rejection kills the server.

title: No observability / logging framework
detection_signal: Using console.log/print instead of structured logging (no winston/pino/log4j/serilog/zap/logrus import); no log levels; no request tracing (no correlation IDs); no metrics collection
why_demo: When production breaks, the debugging strategy is 'add more print statements and redeploy.' There's no way to trace a request through the system or understand failure patterns.

title: No health check endpoint
detection_signal: No /health, /healthz, /ready, /readiness, /liveness endpoints in route definitions; no Kubernetes liveness/readiness probes configured
why_demo: Load balancers and orchestrators can't tell if the app is alive. A half-dead process serving 500s stays in rotation while users suffer.

title: Test files have no descriptive names
detection_signal: Test files named test1.py, test2.js, spec.test.ts, or test_file.py with sequential numbering; test function names are test_1, test_2, it('works'), it('test') with no behavior description
why_demo: When a test fails, the name tells you nothing. You have to read the entire test body to understand what broke. Tests as documentation is impossible when names are meaningless.

title: No test isolation / shared mutable state
detection_signal: Tests modify global state without cleanup (no beforeEach/afterEach/tearDown with state reset); shared database without transactions or truncation; process.env mutations without restore; module-level mutable variables changed in tests
why_demo: Tests pass in sequence but fail alone, or pass alone but fail in suite. The test suite is a fragile house of cards where execution order matters. Developers stop trusting test results.

title: No versioning strategy
detection_signal: No CHANGELOG.md; no conventional-commits enforcement; no semantic-release/release-please/standard-version config; version in package.json/pyproject.toml never bumped (still at 0.0.0 or 1.0.0 with > 50 commits)
why_demo: Users and ops can't tell what's deployed. Rolling back to a known-good version is guesswork. Every deploy is 'latest' which could be anything.

title: Tests take too long to run
detection_signal: CI test step duration > 15 minutes for a non-enterprise project; no test parallelization; no test splitting in CI; no test caching; tests run serially when they could run concurrently
why_demo: Slow tests mean developers skip them. When running the full suite takes 20 minutes, developers push without running tests and rely on CI. Feedback loops break. Productivity tanks.

title: No database migration tooling
detection_signal: SQL schema changes applied by running raw SQL files or modifying CREATE TABLE manually; no Flyway/Alembic/Prisma Migrate/Knex migrations/ActiveRecord migrations; no migrations/ or db/migrate/ directory
why_demo: Schema changes are manual, error-prone, and undocumented. There's no way to reliably apply the same schema to dev, CI, and production. Rolling back a bad migration means manual SQL surgery.

title: No containerization for reproducible builds
detection_signal: No Dockerfile for application; deployment assumes specific host OS/packages installed; build instructions are 'install X, then Y, then run Z'; no multi-stage Docker build
why_demo: The deployment environment is a snowflake — hand-configured, undocumented, and irreplaceable. If the server dies, rebuilding takes hours of guessing which packages were installed.

title: Test assertions are too weak
detection_signal: Tests with no assertions (just calling a function); assertions only check truthiness (expect(result).toBeTruthy(), assert response); snapshot-only tests without specific value checks; assert_called_once without assert_called_with
why_demo: The test proves the code runs without crashing, nothing more. A function returning garbage passes the test as long as it's truthy. These tests catch zero real bugs.

title: No mutation testing
detection_signal: No Stryker, pitest, mutmut, cargo-mutants, or go-mutesting config; no mutation score reported anywhere
why_demo: Test coverage of 80% sounds great until mutation testing reveals half the tests would pass even with bugs introduced. Coverage measures lines hit, not assertions made. Mutation testing catches weak tests.

title: Catch-all exception handling in production code
detection_signal: Bare 'except:' or 'except Exception' with no re-raise; 'catch (e)' with empty block or only comment; 'catch {}' with no logging; rescue => e with only 'nil'; frequency > 3 in production code
why_demo: Swallowed exceptions hide bugs. The code silently continues with corrupted state. Everything looks fine until the data is garbage and nobody knows when or where it went wrong.

title: No contract testing for APIs
detection_signal: No Pact, Dredd, Schemathesis, Prism, or OpenAPI validation testing; API schemas defined but not enforced against in tests; no request/response validation in integration tests
why_demo: Frontend and backend teams have different assumptions about the API contract. Changes to the API break consumers silently. The contract exists only in documentation that's already stale.

title: No performance / load testing
detection_signal: No k6, JMeter, Gatling, Locust, Artillery, or wrk config; no benchmarks directory; no performance regression testing in CI; no load test scripts
why_demo: Nobody knows if the system handles 100 concurrent users or 10. Performance is assumed, not measured. The demo worked with one user. Production has a thousand.

title: No database for testing / tests use production DB
detection_signal: Test configuration points to production database URL; no separate test database; no SQLite in-memory for tests; DATABASE_URL in test env equals production; no testcontainers usage
why_demo: Tests can corrupt production data. A forgotten DELETE FROM users in a test setup script nukes real user data. This isn't demo-grade — it's a ticking time bomb.

title: Git history rewritten or squashed to hide mistakes
detection_signal: Linear git history with no merge commits (always squash-merge OR rebase); OR very few commits relative to project size; OR commit messages are all 'update'/'fix'/'wip'
why_demo: When things go wrong, there's no history to bisect. Every commit is a black box. 'git bisect' is useless when every commit is a massive squash.

title: No .gitignore or incomplete .gitignore
detection_signal: .gitignore missing standard patterns for the tech stack (node_modules, __pycache__, .env, dist/, build/, *.pyc); OR no .gitignore at all; OR .gitignore < 10 lines for a project with > 50 files
why_demo: Build artifacts, dependencies, and IDE configs pollute the repo. node_modules committed to git is the classic signature of a developer who copy-pasted 'git init' without understanding git.

title: No distributed tracing
detection_signal: No OpenTelemetry, Jaeger, Zipkin, Datadog APM, or New Relic integration; no correlation ID middleware; microservice architecture without trace propagation
why_demo: In a distributed system, a single user request touches 5 services. Without tracing, debugging is: 'check each service's logs individually and try to correlate timestamps manually.'

title: No feature flag system
detection_signal: No LaunchDarkly, Unleash, Flagsmith, GrowthBook, or custom feature flag system; features toggled by commenting code or environment variables only; no gradual rollout mechanism
why_demo: Deploying a new feature means deploying to everyone. If it breaks, it breaks for everyone. No gradual rollout, no kill switch, no A/B testing. Ship and pray.

title: CDN / static asset cache busting absent
detection_signal: Static assets referenced without content hashes (src='/app.js' not src='/app.[hash].js'); no webpack/vite/rollup output hashing config; Cache-Control headers missing or set to no-cache on static assets
why_demo: Users get stale JavaScript after deployments. Bug fixes don't take effect until hard refresh. Support tickets pile up: 'I cleared my cache and it works now.'

title: No graceful shutdown handling
detection_signal: No SIGTERM/SIGINT handler; no connection draining; no request completion waiting before exit; Kubernetes pods killed mid-request; no cleanup in process exit handlers
why_demo: During deployment, in-flight requests are dropped. Users see 502 errors on every deploy. The team learns to deploy at 3am to minimize complaints — a bandaid, not a fix.

title: Magic numbers and hardcoded constants
detection_signal: Numeric literals > 3 in non-test production code (excluding 0, 1, -1) with no named constant; hardcoded URLs, ports, timeouts as numbers; regex patterns inline without named groups or comments
why_demo: Nobody knows why the timeout is 3000. Changing it requires finding every occurrence. One is changed, others aren't. The behavior becomes inconsistent and inexplicable.

title: No automated database backup verification
detection_signal: Backup scripts exist but no restore test; no point-in-time recovery config; backup cron job without health check; no backup monitoring/alerting
why_demo: Backups exist but nobody knows if they work. The first restore attempt happens during a real disaster. Murphy's Law guarantees the backup is corrupt when you need it most.

title: No smoke tests post-deployment
detection_signal: Deployment pipeline has no post-deploy verification step; no smoke test hitting health endpoint or critical API; no deployment success criteria; deploy is 'complete' when the process exits
why_demo: The deploy 'succeeded' (the files were copied) but the app is crashing. Users discover the outage before the monitoring does. The team finds out from Twitter complaints.

title: No rate limiting or abuse prevention
detection_signal: No rate limiting middleware (express-rate-limit, django-ratelimit, Rack::Attack); no API key throttling; no request size limits; no CAPTCHA on public endpoints
why_demo: A single bad actor or runaway script can DDoS the service. The demo worked for one user. Production has adversarial traffic.

title: Test doubles not verified
detection_signal: Mocks/stubs created but never asserted against (createMock/stub without verify/expect); sinon.spy without .calledWith assertions; jest.fn() without .toHaveBeenCalledWith; mock objects created and forgotten
why_demo: The mock was set up to prevent errors, not to verify behavior. The function could be calling the dependency with garbage arguments and the test would still pass.

title: No test for authentication / authorization
detection_signal: No tests for: expired tokens, invalid credentials, missing auth headers, insufficient permissions, role-based access, token refresh, session expiry; auth middleware exists but has zero test coverage
why_demo: Authentication is the #1 attack surface. If it's untested, there's a good chance a crafted request bypasses it. Demo code proves the happy login path. It never tests the paths an attacker will use.

title: No input validation testing
detection_signal: API endpoints have no tests for: SQL injection payloads, XSS strings, oversized inputs, missing required fields, wrong types, boundary values; no fuzz testing of input handlers
why_demo: The code assumes inputs are well-formed. An attacker sends '); DROP TABLE users;-- and the demo code happily executes it. Input validation without testing is theater.

title: Config values not externalized
detection_signal: Database URLs, API endpoints, feature toggles, and timeouts hardcoded in source code instead of environment variables or config files; no 12-factor app compliance; config values mixed with business logic
why_demo: Changing a database URL requires a code change and redeployment. Different environments (dev/staging/prod) can't coexist in the same codebase without source modification.

title: No API versioning
detection_signal: API routes have no version prefix (/v1/, /v2/); no Accept header versioning; no API deprecation policy; breaking changes shipped without version bump
why_demo: A backend change breaks all existing clients simultaneously. There's no way to run old and new API versions in parallel during migration. Consumers are forced to update immediately or break.

title: No dependency vulnerability scanning
detection_signal: No 'npm audit'/'pip audit'/'cargo audit'/'bundler-audit' in CI; no Snyk/Socket/GitHub dependency graph; no Dependabot security alerts enabled
why_demo: The project ships with known CVEs. Log4Shell, prototype pollution, path traversal — all present and undetected. The demo works because nobody's attacking it (yet).

title: Test timeouts not configured
detection_signal: No test timeout configuration in test runner config (jest.setTimeout, pytest timeout, mocha --timeout); default timeout used; tests that call external services without timeout
why_demo: A test that hangs blocks the entire CI pipeline. Developers wait 30 minutes before killing the build. Slow tests become acceptable. The test suite degrades into a slow, unreliable mess.

title: Circular dependencies in source code
detection_signal: Import cycle detection tools not configured (no madge --circular, no import-cycle check in eslint, no circular import detection in Python); module A imports B which imports A
why_demo: Circular dependencies cause initialization order bugs, make code untestable in isolation, and create tight coupling that prevents refactoring. They're a design smell that demo code never addresses.

title: No dead code detection
detection_signal: No tree-shaking verification; no dead code detection tools (ts-prune, knip, vulture, deadcode); no unused import linting rules; exported symbols never imported elsewhere
why_demo: Dead code accumulates, confusing developers about what's actually used. Bug fixes in dead code are wasted effort. The codebase grows but the useful surface area shrinks.

title: No accessibility testing
detection_signal: No axe-core, pa11y, jest-axe, @testing-library/jest-dom accessibility matchers; no a11y linting (eslint-plugin-jsx-a11y); no WCAG compliance testing in CI
why_demo: The UI looks great on the demo presenter's screen. It's completely unusable for anyone using a screen reader, keyboard navigation, or high contrast mode.

title: No cross-browser / cross-device testing
detection_signal: No BrowserStack, Sauce Labs, or Playwright cross-browser config; tests only run in one browser (Chrome); no mobile viewport testing; no responsive design verification
why_demo: The demo runs on the developer's Chrome on a 1440p monitor. On Safari, Firefox, mobile, or a different screen size, the layout is broken. 30% of users see a broken UI.

title: No load shedding or circuit breaker
detection_signal: No circuit breaker pattern (no Hystrix, resilience4j, opossum, cockatiel); no bulkhead isolation; no graceful degradation; all-or-nothing service calls
why_demo: One downstream service failure cascades through the entire system. The demo works because all services are healthy. In production, the weakest link brings down everything.

title: No idempotency in write operations
detection_signal: POST/PUT endpoints with no idempotency key handling; payment/charge operations without duplicate detection; no request deduplication; retry logic without idempotent semantics
why_demo: Network retry charges the customer twice. Message queue redelivery creates duplicate records. The demo assumes exactly-once delivery. The real world provides at-least-once at best.

title: No connection pooling or resource management
detection_signal: Database connections created per-request without pooling; HTTP clients created per-call (no axios.create() reuse); file handles not closed (no try-with-resources / using / defer); no connection pool config
why_demo: Works fine with one request. Under load, it exhausts connections, file descriptors, or memory. The demo had one user. Production has a thousand concurrent.

title: No i18n / localization support
detection_signal: Hardcoded user-facing strings in source code (no i18n library like i18next, vue-i18n, react-intl, gettext); date/number formatting using locale-specific defaults; no RTL layout support
why_demo: The demo works in English for the developer's locale. The moment it needs to support another language or region, every string needs extraction. Date formats break, text overflows, RTL languages are unreadable.

title: No automated regression testing on PR
detection_signal: CI pipeline runs only on push to main (not on PR); no PR-triggered test suite; no status checks on pull requests; tests run manually before merge
why_demo: Regressions are discovered after merge, not before. The PR is approved based on code review alone — the reviewer has no automated confidence that the change doesn't break existing functionality.

title: No commit message conventions
detection_signal: No commitlint config; no conventional-commits enforcement; commit messages are 'fix', 'update', 'asdf', 'wip', 'temp'; git log shows no consistent format
why_demo: Git history is noise. Generating changelogs is impossible. 'What changed in v1.2.3?' requires reading every diff. The commit log tells no story about why changes were made.

title: Tests use real third-party API keys
detection_signal: Test files or test config contain real API keys (Stripe sk_live_*, AWS AKIA*, SendGrid SG.*, Twilio); .env.test committed with real keys; no sandbox/test mode for third-party services
why_demo: Running tests charges real money, sends real emails, or modifies real data. CI running on every push triggers real API calls with real billing. One test run could cost hundreds.

title: No deterministic test ordering
detection_signal: Tests rely on execution order (test B assumes test A ran first and modified shared state); no --randomize or --shuffle flag in test config; test files named with numbers to control order
why_demo: The test suite only works in one specific order. Randomize it and half the tests fail. This means the tests share hidden state and can't be parallelized.

title: No architecture decision records
detection_signal: No docs/adr/ or docs/decisions/ directory; no adr-tools config; architectural decisions undocumented; README references no design docs
why_demo: Why was Redis chosen over PostgreSQL for caching? Why is the auth flow async? Nobody knows. Six months later, someone rewrites it 'better' and reintroduces the exact problems the original design solved.

title: No development environment scripts
detection_signal: No Makefile, package.json scripts, docker-compose.dev.yml, or devcontainer config for local setup; setup requires reading a 50-step wiki page; no 'make dev' or 'npm run dev' one-command setup
why_demo: Setting up the project takes a day of 'install this, configure that, run this other thing.' New developers waste a day on environment setup instead of contributing. Tribal knowledge required.

title: No database indexing strategy
detection_signal: No CREATE INDEX statements in migrations; no EXPLAIN ANALYZE in test setup; ORM queries without index hints for common access patterns; no database performance monitoring
why_demo: The demo database has 100 rows. Queries are instant. Production has 10 million rows. Every list endpoint becomes a full table scan. The app grinds to a halt under real data volume.

title: No log level management
detection_signal: All logging at same level (all DEBUG or all INFO); no runtime log level configuration; no structured logging (JSON format); log output mixed with stdout noise
why_demo: In production, either you drown in DEBUG noise or can't see what you need because everything is INFO. There's no way to increase verbosity for a specific component during an incident.

title: No connection / request timeout configuration
detection_signal: HTTP clients created without timeout (axios/requests/fetch without timeout option); database connections without connect_timeout; external service calls with no deadline; default timeout (often 30s+ or infinite)
why_demo: A hung downstream service causes a thread/connection leak. Under load, every thread is waiting on a dead service. The demo never hits this because it never makes a request to a slow service.

title: No automated code formatting enforcement
detection_signal: Prettier/black/rustfmt/gofmt configured but not enforced in CI or pre-commit; formatter config exists but 'format check' is not a CI step; format is optional
why_demo: Formatting is a suggestion, not a rule. PRs have formatting noise mixed with real changes. Some files are formatted, others aren't. The codebase has no visual consistency.

title: No test for concurrency / race conditions
detection_signal: Concurrent code (async/await, goroutines, threads, multiprocessing) with no concurrency-specific tests; no stress tests for shared mutable state; no deadlock detection tools; no race detector flags
why_demo: The code is single-threaded in development but multi-threaded in production. Race conditions never appear in tests because tests don't exercise concurrent paths. They show up at 1000 requests/second.

title: No migration rollback testing
detection_signal: Database migrations have no down/rollback function; migration tests don't verify rollback; no test that runs migrate up then migrate down then migrate up again
why_demo: A bad migration in production has no recovery path. You either fix forward (hoping) or restore from backup (losing data). The demo assumes migrations always succeed.

title: No API rate limit testing
detection_signal: Rate limiting configured but no tests verify: limit threshold, window reset, different limit tiers, concurrent client behavior, 429 response format, Retry-After header
why_demo: Rate limiting works in the demo but nobody verified the edge cases. Does it reset correctly? Does it count correctly under concurrent requests? Is the 429 response properly formatted? Unknown.

title: No error message quality testing
detection_signal: Error responses contain stack traces, internal paths, or SQL queries (grep for 'Error:', 'at /', 'SELECT', 'FROM' in error response tests); no test that error messages are user-safe; no error response schema validation
why_demo: Production errors leak internals. The demo shows a friendly error page. In production, the 500 response contains the full stack trace and database query, giving attackers a roadmap.

title: No health dependency checking
detection_signal: Health endpoint returns 200 without checking downstream dependencies (database, cache, message queue, external API); health check is a static 'ok' response
why_demo: The health check says 'healthy' while the database is down. Load balancers keep routing traffic to a dead node. The health check is a lie.

title: No test for graceful degradation
detection_signal: No tests for behavior when external services are unavailable; no fallback mechanism testing; no test for cached responses when source is down; all-or-nothing service dependencies
why_demo: When the recommendation service is down, the entire product page 500s instead of showing products without recommendations. The demo assumes all services are always available.

title: No data validation at API boundaries
detection_signal: API endpoints accept request bodies without schema validation (no Joi/Zod/Yup/Pydantic/Marshmallow/Ajv); request params used directly in queries without sanitization; no input sanitization middleware
why_demo: The API trusts the client completely. Send a string where a number is expected, add extra fields, nest objects 100 levels deep — the server accepts it all and either crashes or stores garbage.

title: No monitoring alerts configured
detection_signal: No PagerDuty, OpsGenie, Datadog, or Grafana alerting config; no CloudWatch/GCP alert policies; monitoring dashboards exist but no alerts; error rate thresholds not defined
why_demo: Monitoring without alerting is a dashboard nobody looks at. The error rate can climb to 50% at 2am and nobody knows until morning standup. Demo projects have dashboards; production needs alarms.

title: No integration test for payment flows
detection_signal: Payment processing code exists but no integration tests with Stripe/PayPal/payment provider sandbox; no test for: successful charge, declined card, network timeout during charge, webhook verification, refund flow
why_demo: Money is involved. Untested payment code can double-charge customers, fail silently, or leak card details. This isn't demo-grade — it's business-ending-grade.

title: No test for authorization matrix
detection_signal: RBAC/permission system exists but tests only verify 'admin can access admin page'; no test matrix for: user A accessing user B's resources, expired roles, permission inheritance, default permissions
why_demo: The demo shows admin sees admin stuff and user sees user stuff. Nobody tested that user can't access admin endpoints by changing the URL. Authorization bypass is the most common vulnerability.

title: No chaos engineering or fault injection
detection_signal: No Chaos Monkey, Litmus, Gremlin, or Toxiproxy config; no fault injection testing; no network partition simulation; no disk-full simulation
why_demo: The system works perfectly when everything is healthy. Nobody knows what happens when a disk fills up, network latency spikes, or a node goes down. Production will teach you — the hard way.
